entry.title: 7 questions we had going into Config Leadership Collective
entry.summary: We showed up to Leadership Collective hoping to learn how leaders are guiding their teams through change, what expertise means now, and how they're keeping quality high as the pace picks up. Here's what we heard.

entry.title: Got skills? Make the Figma agent a better collaborator
entry.summary: From sharing your best thinking to learning from your team’s go-to prompts, here’s what skills unlock in the Figma agent.

entry.title: Start Anywhere, a magazine by Figma
entry.summary: New tools and materials make it easier to build anything and start anywhere. The question now is, where do you want to go?

entry.title: Meet the artists behind Figma’s Season 5 collection
entry.summary: Figma's Season 5 merch is full of surprises—smiling cubes, a tomato with arms, wooden shapes painted in gouache. We asked the eight artists behind the collection how they work, what inspires them, and what they created.

entry.title: Config 2026: New materials, new tools and a more expressive canvas
entry.summary: Push past what you thought was possible with code layers, Figma Motion, shaders, generative plugins and Weave tools, all on the canvas.

entry.title: Code on the Figma canvas
entry.summary: With code layers in Figma Design, you can explore multiple directions with code, side by side with your team.

entry.title: Connecting Figma and Weave
entry.summary: Today, we’re announcing new ways your Weave creative workflows can live alongside your Figma frames.

entry.title: Introducing Figma Motion: Your canvas now has a timeline
entry.summary: Motion now lives on the canvas—in the same file as your components, your variables, and your team—so your designs can come to life from day one.

entry.title: Figma’s design agent, now with custom tools and greater context
entry.summary: Prompting gets you started. Custom tools, real context, and skills take the design agent further—so what ends up on the canvas actually looks like you.

entry.title: Figma's 2026 AI report: Can AI help us collaborate better?
entry.summary: AI used to be a solo act. According to our research, it’s not anymore.

entry.title: Principles in motion
entry.summary: When you’re designing motion, you’re designing with time. Understanding the mechanics turns movement into meaning.

entry.title: What does the future of software look like?
entry.summary: Our community imagines how AI might bring about more human ways to interact with software.

entry.title: Speaking the language of color
entry.summary: The Pantone Color Institute explains how brands can use color to shape meaning and drive trends.

entry.title: 4 ways we’re using our MCP server at Figma
entry.summary: The Figma MCP server reaches further across the platform than it ever has. From updating a living deck to shipping a design to production—here's what that looks like in practice.

entry.title: You never stop cultivating taste
entry.summary: Having the right tools can only take you so far. To become a master of your craft, you need to hone a point of view.

entry.title: 4 new ways to go from idea to product with AI tools
entry.summary: AI tools are changing how teams build products—from where they start to what carries through to production. Here's what that shift looks like across four organizations.

entry.title: What matters when anyone can build
entry.summary: If AI can make anyone a product builder, the real edge is knowing what’s worth shipping.

entry.title: What the design-to-code loop unlocks
entry.summary: With work moving more fluidly between code and canvas, workflows aren’t just changing—they’re converging.

entry.title: Workflow lab: Expanding the canvas with Figma MCP
entry.summary: As teams build faster, design and code can drift apart just as quickly. This workflow shows how to close that gap—bringing real product states onto the canvas so designers can shape what actually ships.

entry.title: How to design agentic tools for work
entry.summary: The Gemini Enterprise team shares their approach to making complex, multi-agent workflows feel simple, intuitive, and trustworthy.

entry.title: FigJam is now your coding agent’s whiteboard too
entry.summary: Agents are changing your code faster than your team can follow. Now you can close that gap with new MCP skills, architecture layouts, and more in FigJam.

entry.title: The TL;DR on MCP: Why context matters and how to put it to work
entry.summary: Figma’s MCP server brings your design decisions into the tools where code gets written—so what gets built actually matches what was designed. Here’s what that unlocks for everyone who builds products.

entry.title: Agents, meet the Figma canvas
entry.summary: Starting today, you can use AI agents to design directly on the Figma canvas. And with skills, you can guide agents with context about your team’s decisions and intent.

entry.title: Design’s influence is expanding, and here’s why that feels hard
entry.summary: As AI accelerates creation and raises expectations, designers are navigating a field that’s bigger, faster, and more demanding than ever.

entry.title: 5 design skills to sharpen in the AI era
entry.summary: Our latest research highlights the skills designers need to meet the moment.

entry.title: Building frontend UIs with Codex and Figma
entry.summary: With Codex to Figma, teams can bring real, running interfaces into Figma to explore, refine, and make decisions together, then bring it back to Codex with design context intact.

entry.title: State of the Designer 2026: Designers are leaning into the messy middle
entry.summary: Our State of the Designer report explores how designers around the world are upleveling their skills, keeping craft high, and turning new pressures into creative momentum.
